mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates a robust economic expansion, which typically translates into higher corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of GDP growth and equity returns
failure_condition: If real GDP growth consistently remains positive but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a certain threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains above 4.5% for 6 consecutive months and S&P 500 20-day returns are predominantly positive (e.g., > 60% positive instances).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong payroll employment growth and a declining unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market, which boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, positively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over the short term.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.1% AND UNRATE 3-month average change < -0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:strong labor market conditions in early 2006
failure_condition: If both PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline conditions are met, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization. In an environment of moderate inflation, this signals sustainable economic expansion, supporting S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth > 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 3.5%
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is strong and CPIAUCSL growth is moderate, but S&P 500 20-day returns show no significant positive trend over a 4-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in housing starts signals a weakening in the housing market and broader consumer confidence, which can spill over into other sectors of the economy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average change < -10% from prior 3-month average
failure_condition: If HOUST declines significantly for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of the subsequent observations.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate indicates full employment conditions, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, providing a generally positive backdrop for equity markets over longer periods.
failure_condition: If UNRATE remains below 5% for 12 consecutive months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative on average over that period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation coupled with increasing federal funds rate suggests an aggressive monetary tightening cycle. This combination typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate margins, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 3.0% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25pp
evidence_refs: fact:inflationary pressures and Fed hikes in 2006
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS conditions are met, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in retail sales growth indicates weakening consumer demand, which directly impacts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to negative sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending trends are key for economic health
failure_condition: If RSAFS YoY growth falls below 2.0% for two consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive on average over the subsequent 6 months.
